metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4073
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1A: DCV secretion from cholinergic motoneurons was assayed using a transgenic strain (nuIs183) expressing the proneuropeptide NLP-21 tagged to YFP exclusively in dorsally projecting cholinergic motoneurons. This strain has been successfully used to assay DCV biogenesis and release. At the trans-Golgi, the proneuropeptide NLP-21-YFP fusion is packaged into DCVs, where it is subsequently processed into active neuropeptides and soluble YFP. YFP-labeled DCVs are then transported to axonal release sites. After fusion, the soluble YFP is released into the body cavity. Here, it is constitutively endocytosed by macrophage-like cells, called coelomocytes. The YFP fluorescence in coelomocytes can, therefore, be used as an indirect readout for DCV release.

Figure 3A: the coelomocyte YFP intensities were almost abolished in the two tbc-4 deletion alleles, tbc-4 (ok2928) and tbc-4 (tm3255), by 94.64 ± 2.10% and 95.82 ± 1.39%, respectively. Neuron-specific expression of tbc-4 in tbc-4 (ok2928) strain restored DCV exocytosis. Catalytically inactive TBC-4 R155A were unable to restore DCV exocytosis.

Figure 3F: TBC-4 colocalises with Rab-10 in VNC neurons. The paper proposes that tbc-4 might be responsible for regulating the function of Rab10 in DCV exocytosis.

20/01/2021 Dnyanada: The paper shows that Rab10 is involved in dense-core vesicle exocytosis, it also demonstrates that TBC4 is a putative GAP for Rab10. The term "regulation of ..." was chosen as TBC4 regulates dense-core vesicle exocytosis via its GAP activity on Rab10.. Experimental description: The catalytic arginine finger of TBC-4 was mutated to alanine to generate tbc-4 R155A. Wild-type tbc-4, tbc-4 (377-825), and the aforementioned tbc-4 R155A mutant were cloned into pGADT7 as prey. The tested plasmids were mixed to a final concentration of 100 ng/μL along with pbluescript. These mixtures were microinjected into C. elegans to generate transgenic lines as described previously (PMID: 20364145).
The tbc-4 (ok2928) and tbc-4(tm3255) were as TBC mutant-strains in the study. The corresponding worm gene is WBGene00020770 (eat-17).
